forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c180
+title: ui-state-changed must use emit_to(window_label), not global emit
+---
+ROOT CAUSE (keystone). `emit_ui_state_change_if_needed` in apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs used global `app.emit` / `window.emit` (Emitter trait). In Tauri v2 that does NOT reach the dynamically-created board webviews' `listen("ui-state-changed")` subscribers. Proof: spatial focus uses the SAME global `listen("focus-changed")` and WORKS — because its emit uses `emit_to(window_label, ...)` (command_services.rs:84). The per-board notification forwarder also uses emit_to (commands.rs:2733).
+
+Fix: emit per-window via `app.emit_to(label, "ui-state-changed", payload)` for every webview window.
+
+This is the keystone behind: `:` palette never opens, inspector won't close (x), Esc won't dismiss, double-click card doesn't inspect live. All depend on ui-state-changed reaching the webview.
+
+Status: implemented, pending runtime verification (frontend should log `[ui-state-changed] received`).
\ No newline at end of file

Proof: spatial focus uses the SAME global `listen("focus-changed")` and WORKS — because its emit uses `emit_to(window_label, ...)` (command_services.rs:84). The per-board notification forwarder also uses emit_to (commands.rs:2733).
-
-Fix: emit per-window via `app.emit_to(label, "ui-state-changed", payload)` for every webview window.
-
-This is the keystone behind: `:` palette never opens, inspector won't close (x), Esc won't dismiss, double-click card doesn't inspect live. All depend on ui-state-changed reaching the webview.
-
-Status: implemented, pending runtime verification (frontend should log `[ui-state-changed] received`).
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,10 +5,14 @@
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: ui-state-changed must use emit_to(window_label), not global emit
 ---
-ROOT CAUSE (keystone). `emit_ui_state_change_if_needed` in apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs used global `app.emit` / `window.emit` (Emitter trait). In Tauri v2 that does NOT reach the dynamically-created board webviews' `listen("ui-state-changed")` subscribers. Proof: spatial focus uses the SAME global `listen("focus-changed")` and WORKS — because its emit uses `emit_to(window_label, ...)` (command_services.rs:84). The per-board notification forwarder also uses emit_to (commands.rs:2733).
+ROOT CAUSE (keystone). `emit_ui_state_change_if_needed` in apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs used global `app.emit` / `window.emit` (Emitter trait). In Tauri v2 that does NOT reach the dynamically-created board webviews' `listen("ui-state-changed")` subscribers. Proof: spatial focus uses the SAME global `listen("focus-changed")` and WORKS — because its emit uses `emit_to(window_label, ...)` (command_services.rs TauriFocusEventSink::emit). The per-board notification forwarder also uses emit_to.

 Fix: emit per-window via `app.emit_to(label, "ui-state-changed", payload)` for every webview window.

 This is the keystone behind: `:` palette never opens, inspector won't close (x), Esc won't dismiss, double-click card doesn't inspect live. All depend on ui-state-changed reaching the webview.

-Status: implemented, pending runtime verification (frontend should log `[ui-state-changed] received`).
\ No newline at end of file
+Status: IMPLEMENTED + TEST-BACKED. Per-window delivery is now factored behind a `UiStateEventSink` seam (mirrors TauriFocusEventSink); `AppHandle` is the production impl via `emit_to`, and `emit_ui_state_change_to_windows(sink, labels, kind, state)` loops one targeted emit per window label. Unit tests (commands.rs):
+- `ui_state_change_emits_to_every_window_label` — with two windows open, BOTH labels receive a targeted emit_to("ui-state-changed"); RED-proven against a simulated global broadcast (recorded [""] instead of [board-a, board-b]), GREEN after the per-window loop.
+- `ui_state_change_per_window_payload_preserves_kind_and_state` — each targeted emit carries the full {kind, state} snapshot.
+
+`cargo test -p kanban-app --bins`: 136 passed, 0 failed. Zero new warnings (only pre-existing dead-code warning on BoardHandle::open in state.rs, unrelated).
\ No newline at end of file

 Fix: emit per-window via `app.emit_to(label, "ui-state-changed", payload)` for every webview window.

-Status: IMPLEMENTED + TEST-BACKED. -- `ui_state_change_per_window_payload_preserves_kind_and_state` — each targeted emit carries the full {kind, state} snapshot.
-
-`cargo test -p kanban-app --bins`: 136 passed, 0 failed. Zero new warnings (only pre-existing dead-code warning on BoardHandle::open in state.rs, unrelated).

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,14 +5,12 @@
 position_ordinal: '8480'
 title: ui-state-changed must use emit_to(window_label), not global emit
 ---
-ROOT CAUSE (keystone). `emit_ui_state_change_if_needed` in apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs used global `app.emit` / `window.emit` (Emitter trait). In Tauri v2 that does NOT reach the dynamically-created board webviews' `listen("ui-state-changed")` subscribers. Proof: spatial focus uses the SAME global `listen("focus-changed")` and WORKS — because its emit uses `emit_to(window_label, ...)` (command_services.rs TauriFocusEventSink::emit). The per-board notification forwarder also uses emit_to.
+REOPENED 2026-06-06 — prior fix did NOT solve the problem and was discarded.

-Fix: emit per-window via `app.emit_to(label, "ui-state-changed", payload)` for every webview window.
+## OWNER CORRECTION
+The command palette STILL DOES NOT OPEN after the per-window `emit_to` change. So `emit_to(window_label)` was NOT the (whole) root cause of the palette/inspector not working. The code (the `UiStateEventSink` seam + tests, and the pre-existing emit_to partial) was reverted to HEAD.

-This is the keystone behind: `:` palette never opens, inspector won't close (x), Esc won't dismiss, double-click card doesn't inspect live. All depend on ui-state-changed reaching the webview.
-
-Status: IMPLEMENTED + TEST-BACKED. Per-window delivery is now factored behind a `UiStateEventSink` seam (mirrors TauriFocusEventSink); `AppHandle` is the production impl via `emit_to`, and `emit_ui_state_change_to_windows(sink, labels, kind, state)` loops one targeted emit per window label. Unit tests (commands.rs):
-- `ui_state_change_emits_to_every_window_label` — with two windows open, BOTH labels receive a targeted emit_to("ui-state-changed"); RED-proven against a simulated global broadcast (recorded [""] instead of [board-a, board-b]), GREEN after the per-window loop.
-- `ui_state_change_per_window_payload_preserves_kind_and_state` — each targeted emit carries the full {kind, state} snapshot.
+Additionally: the command palette opener is MISSING FROM THE OS MENU entirely (see the broader OS-menu effort / card 01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0 and #bug card 01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13).

-`cargo test -p kanban-app --bins`: 136 passed, 0 failed. Zero new warnings (only pre-existing dead-code warning on BoardHandle::open in state.rs, unrelated).
\ No newline at end of file
+## Next step
+Do NOT assume `emit_to` is the fix. Re-investigate from scratch WHY the command palette does not open — both the hotkey path AND why there is no OS-menu affordance to open it. Likely shares a root cause with the navigation-OS-menu command-surfacing problem (commands not reaching the surface that should present them). Focus the OS-menu work first per the owner. TDD, RED first.
\ No newline at end of file

-title: ui-state-changed must use emit_to(window_label), not global emit
+title: Palette won't open / not on OS menu — SUBSUMED by Card A + CRQ6KJ (recommend close)
 ---
+## RECONCILED 2026-06-06 (dedup sweep)
+This card's title ("ui-state-changed must use emit_to") no longer matches its body. Its actual content — "why does the palette not open: hotkey path + missing OS-menu affordance" — is fully covered by two canonical cards:
+- **Card A `01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0`** — palette opener (+ nav.*) on the OS menu, built FROM the CommandService catalogue.
+- **Palette bug `01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13`** — the hotkey/execution open path (the command's `keys` drive the live keymap, a single service owns `palette_open`, single TS plugin command, no Rust impl).
+
+The `emit_to(window_label)` hypothesis in the original title was tried and reverted (owner note below). **Recommend CLOSING this card as subsumed.** Kept (not deleted) because it is owner-authored — surfacing rather than removing. Any unique signal is preserved below.
+
+---
+
 REOPENED 2026-06-06 — prior fix did NOT solve the problem and was discarded.

 ## OWNER CORRECTION

+---
+## RECONCILED 2026-06-06 (dedup sweep)
+This card's title ("ui-state-changed must use emit_to") no longer matches its body. Its actual content — "why does the palette not open: hotkey path + missing OS-menu affordance" — is fully covered by two canonical cards:
+- **Card A `01KTCQFH7AEQDZD0QETSMCMGP0`** — palette opener (+ nav.*) on the OS menu, built FROM the CommandService catalogue.
+- **Palette bug `01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13`** — the hotkey/execution open path (the command's `keys` drive the live keymap, a single service owns `palette_open`, single TS plugin command, no Rust impl).

+
+## OWNER CORRECTION
+The command palette STILL DOES NOT OPEN after the per-window `emit_to` change. So `emit_to(window_label)` was NOT the (whole) root cause of the palette/inspector not working. The code (the `UiStateEventSink` seam + tests, and the pre-existing emit_to partial) was reverted to HEAD.
+

+
